name: FUNCTION_COMBINATIONS_FOR_DOUBLE_HIGH_EXPRESSION_TARGETPAIRS
description: Functional category combinations for double high expression target pairs. Auto-generated from the core-four benchmark and aligned to the validated query-frame Cypher semantics.
cypher:
MATCH (tp:TargetPair)-[:HAS_TARGET]->(t1:Target)-[:DIFFERENTIAL_AND_HIGHLY_EXPRESSED_IN]->(c:Cancer {name: $cancer})
MATCH (tp)-[:HAS_TARGET]->(t2:Target)-[:DIFFERENTIAL_AND_HIGHLY_EXPRESSED_IN]->(c)
WHERE t1 <> t2
OPTIONAL MATCH (t1)-[:FUNCTIONED_AS]->(f1:Functional_of_Target)
OPTIONAL MATCH (t2)-[:FUNCTIONED_AS]->(f2:Functional_of_Target)
RETURN DISTINCT tp.name AS target_pair, collect(DISTINCT f1.name) AS target1_functions, collect(DISTINCT f2.name) AS target2_functions
ORDER BY target_pair
LIMIT 10

examples: For target pairs whose two targets are both highly expressed in LIHC, what functional-of-target combinations do they represent?
For target pairs whose two targets are both highly expressed in LAML, what functional-of-target combinations do they represent?
For target pairs whose two targets are both highly expressed in READ, what functional-of-target combinations do they represent?
